description: Node.js project with deprecated packages, known CVEs, and pre/postinstall hooks
review_checklist: Identify deprecated packages and their replacements
Audit lifecycle scripts (pre/postinstall) for supply-chain exposure

ground_truth.description: left-pad: famously the package whose 2016 unpublishing broke half of npm. Functionality is trivially replaceable with String.prototype.padStart. Continued use is a code smell.
request: officially deprecated in February 2020. No further updates including for security. Use undici, axios, or native fetch.
node-fetch 2.6.1 has CVE-2022-0235 (exfiltration of secure headers in cross-domain redirects) — patched in 2.6.7. Also node-fetch 2.x is in maintenance-only mode; 3.x is ESM-only.
tar 4.4.0 has CVE-2021-32803 (arbitrary file write via symlink) and CVE-2021-37701 (arbitrary file overwrite). Patched in 4.4.18+, 5.0.10+, 6.1.9+.
ws 6.2.0 has CVE-2024-37890 (DoS via prototype pollution). Patched in 8.17.1, 7.5.10. 6.x is no longer maintained.
minimatch 3.0.4 has CVE-2022-3517 (ReDoS). Patched in 3.0.5+ and 5.0+. The 3.x version is widely depended-on but vulnerable to crafted patterns.

preinstall and postinstall scripts execute on every npm install. They run with the developer's full filesystem access. Audit ./scripts/setup.js and ./scripts/finalize.js — these are common locations for supply-chain compromise. Consider running with --ignore-scripts in CI.
